output: {"domain":"api_infrastructure","task":"Validate incoming API request schema and auth tokens, classify the request type and rate-limit tier, then route to the correct microservice.","solution_steps":[{"action":"Extract HTTP headers and body from the incoming request.","expected_outcome":"Structured representation of headers (e.g., Content-Type, Authorization) and raw payload ready for validation."},{"action":"Validate the authentication token using the configured auth provider.","expected_outcome":"Determination of token validity, extraction of user identity, scopes, and expiration status."},{"action":"Parse the request body according to the expected JSON schema for the endpoint.","expected_outcome":"A validated data object or a detailed error report indicating schema violations."},{"action":"Identify the request type (e.g., CRUD operation, webhook, batch job) based on HTTP method and endpoint path.","expected_outcome":"Categorization of the request into a predefined set of types for routing logic."},{"action":"Determine the appropriate rate‑limit tier by evaluating user role, token scopes, and any quota metadata.","expected_outcome":"A rate‑limit bucket assignment (e.g., free, standard, premium) with remaining allowance."},{"action":"Apply rate limiting checks against the chosen tier; if exceeded, generate a 429 response.","expected_outcome":"Either an allowed request proceeding or a throttling error with retry‑after header."},{"action":"Map the classified request type to its target microservice endpoint using the service registry.","expected_outcome":"The exact URL and protocol details for forwarding the request."},{"action":"Forward the validated request (headers, body, auth context) to the selected microservice via HTTP proxy or message bus.","expected_outcome":"Successful delivery of the request with a response captured for downstream handling."}],"quality_self_assessment":{"score":0.92,"justification":"All core steps are covered, error handling and rate limiting logic included;
